name: ImageNet
description: 1.2M+ labeled images across 1,000 classes (Deng et al. 2009); catalyzed AlexNet (2012) and the supervised-vision era.

name: AlexNet
description: 2012 ILSVRC-winning CNN (Krizhevsky, Sutskever, Hinton) whose result depended fundamentally on ImageNet scale.

name: Common Crawl
description: Open web crawl corpus, founded 2007, underlying nearly every open LLM pretraining mix.

name: GPT-3
description: OpenAI 2020 model trained on ~300B filtered tokens; demonstrated in-context learning at scale.

name: The Pile
description: EleutherAI's 825GB open corpus (Gao et al. 2020) across 22 curated sources; substrate for GPT-J, Pythia, and most early open LLMs.

name: C4 (Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus)
description: Raffel et al. 2019/2020 cleaned Common Crawl dump from the T5 paper; established the standard web-filtering recipe.

name: RefinedWeb
description: Penedo et al. 2023 (TII/Falcon); demonstrated heavy dedup + aggressive filtering on web-only matches curated mixes.

name: RedPajama
description: Together AI 2023 open reproduction of LLaMA's 1.2T-token mix; v2 (30T CC tokens with quality signals) followed.

name: FineWeb
description: HuggingFace 2024 15T-token filtered CC corpus with fully documented pipeline and extensive ablations.

name: FineWeb-Edu
description: Llama-3-annotated educational classifier filter reducing FineWeb to 1.3T high-quality tokens with large MMLU/ARC gains.

name: FineWeb-2
description: HuggingFace Dec 2024 multilingual extension: 20TB across 1,868 language-script pairs, beats CC-100/mC4/CulturaX on 9-language ablations.

name: Dolma
description: AI2's 3T-token open corpus (2024) with fully documented pipeline; powers OLMo.

name: DCLM (DataComp for Language Models)
description: Li et al. 2024 testbed with 240T CC pool and fixed training recipe; DCLM-Baseline reaches 64% MMLU at 7B/2.6T.

name: Nemotron-CC
description: NVIDIA 2024 ~6.3T-token CC pipeline using multi-classifier ensemble filtering.

name: DoReMi
description: Xie et al. 2023 group-DRO method for learning domain mixture weights via a proxy model.

name: LAION-5B
description: Schuhmann et al. 2022 open CLIP-filtered image-text pair dataset (5.85B pairs); precondition for Stable Diffusion.

name: COYO-700M
description: Kakao 2022 open 700M image-text pairs; alternative/complement to LAION.

name: DataComp
description: Gadre et al. 2023 benchmark for image-text curation with fixed CLIP training recipe; analog of DCLM for multimodal.

name: OBELICS
description: HuggingFace 2023 open interleaved image-text web documents (141M) enabling IDEFICS and open multimodal models.

name: Self-Instruct
description: Wang et al. Dec 2022 pipeline bootstrapping 52K instructions from a seed LM; foundational for distilled instruction tuning.

name: Alpaca
description: Stanford March 2023 52K instruction dataset generated from text-davinci-003 at ~$600 cost.

name: ShareGPT / Vicuna
description: User-shared ChatGPT conversations (2023) used to train LMSYS's Vicuna; popularized distilled chat tuning.

name: Phi series
description: Microsoft's small models (Phi-1 through Phi-4, 2023–2024) trained heavily on synthetic textbook-style data; results strong on benchmarks, contested on Arena-style evaluation.

name: Orca / Orca 2
description: Microsoft 2023 distilled datasets using GPT-4 explanation traces on FLAN collections.

name: Cosmopedia
description: HuggingFace 2024 open 25B-token synthetic textbook-style corpus; open analog of Phi's data.

name: STaR
description: Zelikman et al. 2022 Self-Taught Reasoner; generate CoT, filter by correct answer, fine-tune; foundational for reasoning distillation.

name: DeepSeek-R1
description: DeepSeek Jan 2025 reasoning model; R1-Zero uses pure RL with verifiable rewards, R1 adds SFT, R1-distilled fine-tunes Qwen/Llama on 800K traces.

name: OpenAI o1
description: OpenAI Sept 2024 reasoning model using RL over long CoTs; training data and recipe remain closed.

name: OpenThoughts / OpenThinker
description: Open 2025 reasoning-trace corpus (OpenThoughts3: 1.2M QwQ-32B traces) enabling open R1-parity distilled models.

name: Shumailov et al. model collapse
description: Nature 2024 paper showing recursive training on self-generated data degrades models; Gerstgrasser et al. 2024 showed accumulation (not replacement) avoids collapse.

name: InstructGPT
description: Ouyang et al. OpenAI 2022; established SFT→RM→PPO RLHF pipeline.

name: Anthropic HH-RLHF
description: Bai et al. April 2022; ~170K human preference pairs on helpfulness and harmlessness; canonical open RLHF dataset.

name: Constitutional AI / RLAIF
description: Anthropic Dec 2022; model-written critiques substitute for human harmlessness labels — first scaled AI-feedback result.

name: OpenAssistant / OASST
description: LAION April 2023; 161K community-sourced messages across 66K conversation trees.

name: UltraFeedback
description: Cui et al. Oct 2023; 64K prompts × 4 responses × GPT-4 multi-attribute scoring; default DPO corpus.

name: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)
description: Rafailov et al. May 2023; single-loss alternative to PPO that collapsed the RLHF pipeline and reshaped post-training economics.

name: HelpSteer / HelpSteer2
description: NVIDIA 2023–2024 multi-attribute preference datasets.

name: Tulu 2/3
description: AI2 2023–2024 fully documented open post-training datasets; Tulu 3 introduced RLVR (RL with verifiable rewards).

name: RewardBench
description: Lambert et al. March 2024; standardized reward-model evaluation; exposed length-bias in many open RMs.

name: PRM800K
description: Lightman et al. 2023 OpenAI; 800K step-level process-reward labels on MATH problems.

name: The Stack (v1/v2)
description: BigCode/HuggingFace 2022 and 2024; permissively-licensed code corpus (v2: 900B tokens) underlying StarCoder.

name: HumanEval
description: Chen et al. 2021 OpenAI; 164 Python programming problems; universal code benchmark.

name: GSM8K
description: Cobbe et al. OpenAI 2021; 8.5K grade-school math word problems; cornerstone of reasoning evaluation.

name: MATH dataset
description: Hendrycks et al. 2021; 12.5K competition-level math problems with step-by-step solutions.

name: MS COCO
description: Lin et al. 2014; 330K images with 2.5M object instances, segmentation masks, and captions; defined dense vision supervision.

name: Visual Genome
description: Krishna et al. 2016; dense scene graphs with objects, attributes, and relations.

name: Segment Anything / SA-1B
description: Kirillov et al. Meta 2023; 11M images with 1.1B masks generated via model-in-the-loop annotation flywheel.

name: Ego4D
description: Meta 2021; 3,670 hours of egocentric video; foundational for embodied-AI pretraining.

name: Open X-Embodiment / RT-X
description: Oct 2023 consortium dataset across 22 robot embodiments and ~1M trajectories from 34 labs; enabled cross-embodiment transfer.

name: DROID
description: Khazatsky et al. RSS 2024; 76K teleoperated trajectories, 350 hours, 564 scenes, 86 tasks, 13 institutions, Franka Panda-standardized, CC-BY-4.0.

name: Protein Data Bank (PDB)
description: RCSB repository of experimental protein structures (1971–); foundational substrate for AlphaFold.

name: AlphaFold 2/3
description: DeepMind 2021/2024; solved protein structure prediction by combining PDB with MSA-derived co-evolution signals.

name: ROOTS / BLOOM
description: BigScience 2022; 1.6TB multilingual corpus across 46 natural and 13 programming languages; trained BLOOM.

name: NLLB-200
description: Meta 2022 No Language Left Behind; MT across 200 languages with accompanying parallel data.

name: MADLAD-400
description: Google 2023; 3T tokens across 419 languages with document-level audits.

name: Aya Collection
description: Cohere for AI 2024; 513M multilingual instruction instances across 114 languages, including 204K human-curated.

name: Bartz v. Anthropic
description: N.D. Cal., Judge Alsup, June 2025; training fair use but piracy-sourced library not; class-certified, settled Sept 2025.

name: Kadrey v. Meta
description: N.D. Cal., Judge Chhabria, June 2025; fair-use ruling on Llama training with market-harm emphasis.

name: NYT v. OpenAI/Microsoft
description: Filed Dec 2023; motion to dismiss largely denied March 2025; proceeding to trial; includes contested preservation order.

name: Getty v. Stability AI
description: UK and US cases against Stable Diffusion training on Getty-watermarked images; key test of image-copyright claims.

name: Re-LAION-5B
description: Aug 2024 re-release after Dec 2023 Stanford CSAM report; 2,236 links removed with IWF/C3P partnership.

name: Data Provenance Initiative
description: Longpre et al. 2023–2024; audited licenses on 1,800+ datasets; 'Consent in Crisis' documented rapid robots.txt closure.

name: Common Pile
description: EleutherAI 2024–2025 open-licensed-only corpus; reaches ~80% of mixed-corpus performance at 7B scale.

name: OpenAI
description: Frontier lab whose GPT series, InstructGPT, Codex, and o1 defined several data paradigms; extensive 2024–2025 licensing deals.
